# Rmd notebooks extension example

In this `.Rmd` file we will simply run a couple of chunks to showcase the extension.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 8))
```



Txt/HTML content gets printed inline

```{r}
data(penguins)
head(penguins)
```



Figures are also shown inline, and parameters can be controlled in the same way
as `knitr` options by editing the bracketed content:

```{r, fig.width=8, fig.height=6, dpi=90}
ggplot(penguins) + 
    aes(flipper_len, fill=species) + 
    geom_histogram(
        aes(y = after_stat(density)),
        position = "identity",
        alpha = 0.35,
        bins = 30
    ) +
    labs(x="Flipper length (mm)", y="Frequency") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
        "Adelie" = "#F4A340",
        "Chinstrap" = "#B36AE8",
        "Gentoo" = "#4FAEAE")
)
```
